theory.intro: A **table of values** lists matching $x$ and $y$ values for a linear equation. To complete one, substitute each $x$-value into the equation and evaluate to find its $y$-value.
facts: Each column pairs an $x$-value with the $y$-value the equation gives for it.
**Substitute each $x$-value in brackets**, so a negative keeps its sign: for $y = -3x$ at $x = -2$, $y = -3(-2) = 6$.
A **fractional coefficient** works the same way: for $y = \frac{x}{2}$ at $x = 6$, $y = \frac{6}{2} = 3$.
Work left to right, filling one column at a time.
steps: Take the next $x$-value from the table.
Substitute it into the equation, in brackets.
Record $y$ in that column, then repeat.

foundation.question_text:
Complete the table of values for $y = 2x$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

Complete the table of values for $y = 4x + 3$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

Complete the table of values for $y = 2x + 5$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

Complete the table of values for $y = x + 2$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

Complete the table of values for $y = 5x + 2$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

development.question_text:
Complete the table of values for $y = 3x + 2$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & -3 & -2 & -1 & 0 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

Complete the table of values for $y = -3x + 2$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

Complete the table of values for $y = -4x + 3$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & -1 & 0 & 1 & 2 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

Complete the table of values for $y = \frac{x}{2} + 3$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & 0 & 2 & 4 & 6 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

Complete the table of values for $y = \frac{x}{3} + 1$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & 0 & 3 & 6 & 9 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

mastery.question_text:
Complete the table of values for $y = -3x - 1$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & -2 & -1 & 0 & 1 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

Complete the table of values for $y = \frac{x}{3} - 1$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & -6 & -3 & 0 & 3 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$

Complete the table of values for $y = \frac{x}{2} + 3$.
$\begin{array}{c|cccc} x & -3 & -1 & 1 & 3 \\ \hline y & & & & \end{array}$
